headlines.title: Nigeria Needs New Export Markets as UAE's Exit Rattles OPEC
headlines.summary: Nigeria should market its crude oil to new buyers as the UAE’s decision to leave OPEC is dislocating the balance that the cartel and the OPEC+ group have been seeking for years, according to Wole Ogunsanya, chairman of the Petroleum Technology Association of Nigeria (PETAN). The

headlines.title: Indian Airlines Ask Refiners to Postpone Local Jet Fuel Price Hike
headlines.summary: Major Indian airlines have asked the state oil companies to postpone hikes in jet fuel prices for domestic flights until the Middle East crisis is over, Bloomberg reported on Tuesday, quoting sources with knowledge of the development. headlines.title: Japan and South Korea Deepen Oil Ties as Hormuz Crisis Bites
headlines.summary: The prime ministers of Japan and South Korea have agreed to boost oil supply cooperation, which would involve joint storage, Nikkei reported today, ahead of a meeting between the top officials in South Korea. headlines.title: Australia Turns to China for Emergency Jet Fuel Supplies
headlines.summary: Australia has secured three shipments of jet fuel from China, and more are expected in the coming months as Australia is scrambling to ensure its fuel supply amid the global crunch, and one of its only two refineries is out of operation due to a fire. headlines.title: India Hikes Fuel Prices for the Second Time in a Week
headlines.summary: India’s state fuel traders raised retail prices for the second time in less than a week, as they seek to cushion the blow to margins from soaring crude prices while the war in the Middle East drags on with no resolution in sight. headlines.title: Asia’s Gas Crunch Opens the Door for Australian LNG
headlines.summary: Asian energy importers are likely to develop a stronger - and lasting - taste for Australian natural gas as they get pressured by a Middle East energy commodity supply shortage. headlines.title: Oil Prices Fall After Trump Pauses Planned Iran Strike
headlines.summary: Oil prices dropped in early Asian trade on Tuesday after President Trump said he had paused a planned military strike on Iran to allow negotiations to continue. headlines.title: US Waiver Extension Keeps India’s Russian Crude Lifeline Open

headlines.title: Higher Oil Prices Have Cost U.S. Consumers $45 Billion Since Iran War Began
headlines.summary: As U.S. drivers face the highest Memorial Day gasoline prices in four years, American consumers are paying billions of dollars more on gas and diesel than they did a year ago.
